speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Threescore and ten I can remember well,

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Within the volume of which time I have seen

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Hours dreadful and things strange, but this sore night

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Hath trifled former knowings.

speaker: ROSS
text: Ha, good father,

speaker: ROSS
text: Thou seest the heavens, as troubled with man’s act,

speaker: ROSS
text: Threatens his bloody stage. By th’ clock ’tis day,

speaker: ROSS
text: And yet dark night strangles the traveling lamp.

speaker: ROSS
text: Is ’t night’s predominance or the day’s shame

speaker: ROSS
text: That darkness does the face of earth entomb

speaker: ROSS
text: When living light should kiss it?

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: ’Tis unnatural,

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Even like the deed that’s done. On Tuesday last

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: A falcon, tow’ring in her pride of place,

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Was by a mousing owl hawked at and killed.

speaker: ROSS
text: And Duncan’s horses (a thing most strange and certain),

speaker: ROSS
text: Beauteous and swift, the minions of their race,

speaker: ROSS
text: Turned wild in nature, broke their stalls, flung out,

speaker: ROSS
text: Contending ’gainst obedience, as they would

speaker: ROSS
text: Make war with mankind.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: ’Tis said they eat each other.

speaker: ROSS
text: They did so, to th’ amazement of mine eyes

speaker: ROSS
text: That looked upon ’t.

speaker: ROSS
text: Here comes the good Macduff.—

speaker: ROSS
text: How goes the world, sir, now?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Why, see you not?

speaker: ROSS
text: Is ’t known who did this more than bloody deed?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Those that Macbeth hath slain.

speaker: ROSS
text: Alas the day,

speaker: ROSS
text: What good could they pretend?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: They were suborned.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Malcolm and Donalbain, the King’s two sons,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Are stol’n away and fled, which puts upon them

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Suspicion of the deed.

speaker: ROSS
text: ’Gainst nature still!

speaker: ROSS
text: Thriftless ambition, that will ravin up

speaker: ROSS
text: Thine own lives’ means. Then ’tis most like

speaker: ROSS
text: The sovereignty will fall upon Macbeth.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: He is already named and gone to Scone

speaker: MACDUFF
text: To be invested.

speaker: ROSS
text: Where is Duncan’s body?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Carried to Colmekill,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: The sacred storehouse of his predecessors

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And guardian of their bones.

speaker: ROSS
text: Will you to Scone?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: No, cousin, I’ll to Fife.

speaker: ROSS
text: Well, I will thither.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Well, may you see things well done there. Adieu,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Lest our old robes sit easier than our new.

speaker: ROSS
text: Farewell, father.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: God’s benison go with you and with those

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: That would make good of bad and friends of foes.
